Write/edit mutation handling resolves target-path repo context so valid non-main worktree mutations are not misclassified as main-branch edits.
Unsafe or ambiguous cross-repo/cross-worktree mutation attempts remain blocked with clear reasons.
Unit/integration validation covers parent-cwd-on-main with target-worktree-on-branch cases plus ambiguous/unsafe rejection cases.
evidence: Changed files: .pi/agent/extensions/safe-bash.ts, tests/extension-units/safe-bash.test.ts, tests/extension-units/test-utils.ts.

Implemented worktree-aware mutation context in safe-bash: write/edit resolve target path cwd, bounded leading `cd <path> && ...` bash commands classify against the target worktree, and cross-repo/non-repo targets are blocked using git common-dir checks with clear audit reasons.

Expanded safe-bash unit coverage for parent-session-on-main with target-worktree-on-branch write/edit and leading-`cd` bash cases, plus cross-repo and non-repo rejection paths; updated FakePi test utility to model per-cwd branch/common-dir state accurately including explicit null non-repo cases.
